data.title: Is It Time To Reconsider Descartes Systems Group (TSX:DSG) After Its 27% One-Year Drop?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering whether Descartes Systems Group at around $99.49 is attractively priced, or whether they would be paying for growth that may already be reflected in the share price.
  • The stock has returned 2.9% over the last 7 days and 1.6% over the last 30 days, but is still showing a 14.9% decline year to date and a 27.1% decline over the past year.
  • These moves are occurring as investors reassess software names more broadly and pay closer attention to the balance between growth expectations and current valuation. For Descartes, that makes understanding what you are paying for each dollar of its future potential a key part of any decision to watch or avoid the stock for now.
  • Simply Wall St currently scores Descartes Systems Group at 2 out of 6 on its undervaluation checks. The rest of this article will walk through the main valuation approaches used, then finish with a different lens that can help put those numbers into a clearer story.

Descartes Systems Group scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Descartes Systems Group Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to today’s dollars. This gives an estimate of what the business could be worth right now based on those projected cash streams.

For Descartes Systems Group, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach built on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is $259.5 million. Analysts provide explicit estimates for several years, and Simply Wall St extends those with its own projections, reaching an estimated free cash flow of $459.8 million in 2035.

After discounting each of those future cash flows back to today, the DCF output suggests an intrinsic value of about $118.26 per share. Compared with a current share price of around CA$99.49, this implies the stock is trading at a 15.9% discount to that DCF estimate, which indicates that the shares appear undervalued on this specific model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Descartes Systems Group is undervalued by 15.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 8 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Descartes Systems Group.

Approach 2: Descartes Systems Group Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you are paying for each share to the earnings that business is currently generating. It gives a quick sense of how many years of current earnings are effectively embedded in the share price.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth outlook and risk profile. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower, more conservative multiple.

Descartes Systems Group currently trades on a P/E of 37.47x. That is above the Software industry average of 23.14x and also above the peer group average of 67.01x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Descartes is 26.28x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk factors. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers and the broader industry because it attempts to adjust for these company specific drivers. With the current P/E of 37.47x sitting above the Fair Ratio of 26.28x, the shares screen as expensive on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so Narratives are introduced as a simple way to connect your view of Descartes Systems Group’s business to a set of numbers. data.title: Is It Too Late To Reassess Sienna Senior Living (TSX:SIA) After Its Strong 1-Year Rally?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Sienna Senior Living's recent share price puts it on sale or at a premium, the next sections will walk through how that value question can be framed using clear, practical tools.
  • The stock last closed at $22.41, with returns of 3.6% over 7 days, a 3.0% decline over 30 days, 10.1% year to date, 42.8% over 1 year and 110.7% over 5 years, which gives plenty of recent data points for investors to weigh.
  • Alongside these moves, investors have been reacting to ongoing sector themes around ageing populations, senior care models and how operators balance occupancy, care quality and capital needs. For Sienna Senior Living, this context helps frame whether the recent share price performance lines up with the underlying business drivers that matter most to long term holders.
  • Right now, Sienna Senior Living scores 0 out of 6 on Simply Wall St's valuation checks (which you can see in more detail in the current valuation score), and the rest of this article will walk through classic valuation methods before finishing with a broader way to think about what the market might be pricing in.

Sienna Senior Living scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Sienna Senior Living Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of the cash a business could generate in the future and discounts those amounts back to what they might be worth today. It is essentially asking what those future CA$ cash flows are worth in today’s money.

For Sienna Senior Living, the latest twelve month Free Cash Flow (FCF) is a loss of CA$50.59 million. The model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. It starts with this recent FCF and then applies analyst input and extrapolated projections. For example, one analyst input suggests FCF of CA$38 million in 2027, and Simply Wall St extends that path out to 2035 with gradually changing estimates each year.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back and combined with a terminal value, the DCF model produces an estimated intrinsic value of CA$10.09 per share. Compared with the recent share price of CA$22.41, this indicates the stock is 122.2% overvalued based on this method alone.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Sienna Senior Living may be overvalued by 122.2%. Discover 8 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Sienna Senior Living.

Approach 2: Sienna Senior Living Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay per share to the earnings that each share generates. It helps you see how many years of current earnings the market is effectively pricing in.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E depends on how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower one.

Sienna Senior Living currently trades on a P/E of 51.80x. That sits above the Healthcare industry average P/E of 18.95x and above a peer average of 18.78x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio”, which is the P/E you might expect given factors like Sienna Senior Living’s earnings growth profile, its industry, profit margins, market cap and key risks.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for the specific characteristics of the company rather than assuming all Healthcare stocks deserve similar multiples. In this case, the current P/E of 51.80x sits above the Fair Ratio assessment, which points to the shares being overvalued on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 2 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Sienna Senior Living Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives are a tool that let you attach a clear story about Sienna Senior Living’s prospects to concrete numbers like your own fair value estimate and your expectations for future revenue, earnings and margins. You can then link that story to a forecast and finally to a fair value that you can compare with today’s share price on Simply Wall St’s Community page, where Narratives are available to millions of investors and update automatically when fresh information such as news or earnings is added. This can help you decide whether the gap between your Fair Value and the current Price supports buying, holding or selling. Two investors looking at the same company can reasonably arrive at very different fair values based on their own assumptions about things like occupancy, funding conditions and operating efficiency.
